title: ci: make the automation release renovate managed
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?



#### What this PR does / why we need it:


Put the variable in the workflow file like the renovate config expects,
see https://github.com/podman-container-tools/automation/pull/29

To ensure the local runs keep working we need a fall back to parse the
file manually.

Same as the podman change for this which is known to work:
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/commit/8e74c1efd88e39d462e1f4411dc741e5cd9739bb
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/29186

Also manually update the image id for now while at it to safe one CI run.


#### How to verify it

see the change in podman

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
@nalind new conformance test failure, I guess due a docker/buildkit update in the VM.

```
=== RUN   TestConformance/heredoc-copy
    conformance_test.go:763: requesting docker builder BuildKit
    conformance_test.go:545: 
        	Error Trace:	/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:545
        	            				/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:392
        	            				/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:191
        	Error:      	Filesystem contents differ
        	Test:       	TestConformance/heredoc-copy
        	Messages:   	File attributes in both versions have different values:
        	            	File:attr                     Docker        buildah
        	            	/test/humans.txt:mode         0664          0644
        	            	/test/robots.txt:mode         0664          0644
        	            	/test2/robots.txt:mode        0664          0644
        	            	/test2/humans.txt:mode        0664          0644
    conformance_test.go:414: Context "/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/testdata/heredoc"
    conformance_test.go:418: Dockerfile: "/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/testdata/heredoc/Dockerfile.heredoc_copy"
    conformance_test.go:426: Dockerfile contents:
        # syntax=mirror.gcr.io/docker/dockerfile:1.3-labs
        FROM mirror.gcr.io/busybox as one
        RUN echo helloworld > image_file
        FROM mirror.gcr.io/busybox
        RUN echo hello
        # copy two heredoc and one from context
        COPY <<robots.txt <<humans.txt file /test/
        (robots content)
        Long file with random text
        Random line
        HelloWorld
        robots.txt
        (humans content)
        humans.txt
        # copy two heredoc and one from another stage
        COPY --from=one image_file <<robots.txt <<humans.txt /test2/
        (robots content)
        Long file with random text
        Random line
        HelloWorld
        robots.txt
        (humans content)
        humans.txt
```

What is the recommend way to fix these, make buildah use the same file permissions?

> What is the recommend way to fix these, make buildah use the same file permissions?

Unless the daemon is running with a more permissive umask and that's somehow responsible for the change, we'd change the permissions we assign, as you suggest.
Confirmed that this is an intentional change.  The corresponding change here would likely be made at https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/main/imagebuildah/stage_executor.go#L461

looks like this was a bit to much, I guess the permissions are not that consistent on the buildkit side?
```
=== RUN   TestConformance/heredoc-quoting
    conformance_test.go:763: requesting docker builder BuildKit
    conformance_test.go:545: 
        	Error Trace:	/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:545
        	            				/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:392
        	            				/var/tmp/buildah/tests/conformance/conformance_test.go:191
        	Error:      	Filesystem contents differ
        	Test:       	TestConformance/heredoc-quoting
        	Messages:   	File attributes in both versions have different values:
        	            	File:attr                       Docker        buildah
        	            	/add-quoted2.txt:mode           0644          0664
        	            	/copy-quoted4.txt:mode          0644          0664
        	            	/add-unquoted2.txt:mode         0644          0664
        	            	/add-unquoted3.txt:mode         0644          0664
        	            	/add-quoted3.txt:mode           0644          0664
        	            	/add-quoted4.txt:mode           0644          0664
        	            	/add-quoted1.txt:mode           0644          0664
        	            	/add-unquoted1.txt:mode         0644          0664
        	            	/add-unquoted4.txt:mode         0644          0664
        	            	/copy-quoted2.txt:mode          0644          0664
        	            	/copy-unquoted2.txt:mode        0644          0664
        	            	/copy-unquoted3.txt:mode        0644          0664
        	            	/copy-unquoted4.txt:mode        0644          0664
        	            	/copy-quoted1.txt:mode          0644          0664
        	            	/copy-quoted3.txt:mode          0644          0664
        	            	/copy-unquoted1.txt:mode        0644          0664

```

Ah, okay.  I dug into it, and this was a hard-wired value that changed between versions 1.5 and 1.6 of the dockerfile frontend.  Either dropping the `# syntax` line from `tests/conformance/testdata/heredoc/Dockerfile.heredoc_copy`, or updating it to point to version 1.6 or later (the current version is 1.25) of the frontend image, should produce the behavior the tests expected.
